items.back: Chapter 8 procedures apply unless a directive or letter of agreement prescribes different procedures or minima.

items.front: Chapter 8 procedures apply unless a directive or letter of agreement prescribes different procedures or minima.
items.back: 8-1-1: Oceanic ATC service default and exception rule.
JO 7110.65 paragraph 1-1-10, Procedural Letters of Agreement (LOAs).
Use Chapter 8 procedures and minima first. For situations not covered by Chapter 8, apply the provisions in the rest of the Order.

items.front: Use Chapter 8 procedures and minima first. For situations not covered by Chapter 8, apply the provisions in the rest of the Order.
8-1-3: Three conditions for VFR flight plan ops in Oceanic FIRs
items.back: 1. Weather at or above 14 CFR 91.155 minimums. 2. Between sunrise and sunset only. 3. Only within: Miami/Houston/San Juan CTAs below FL180, Oakland FIR less than 100 NM seaward in controlled airspace, or all Oceanic FIR airspace below the CTAs.

items.front: 8-1-3: Weather and time limits for oceanic VFR flight plans
items.back: Weather: at or above 14 CFR 91.155 basic VFR minimums. Time: between sunrise and sunset only.
1. Vertical separation. 2. Horizontal separation (longitudinal or lateral). 3. Radar separation where radar coverage is adequate. At least one must be used.

items.front: Vertical, horizontal (longitudinal/lateral), or radar (where coverage adequate). At least one required.
items.back: 8-1-4: Types of separation in oceanic/offshore procedures.

items.front: 8-1-5: Oceanic control area altitude assignment rule
items.back: Based on flight levels and a standard altimeter setting of 29.92 inches Hg, unless directed and/or charted otherwise.

items.front: 29.92 inches Hg standard, flight levels, unless directed/charted otherwise
